stories.title: Charles Schwab Challenge R1 Recap — Six Share Lead at Colonial
stories.angle: Six co-leaders create wide-open field at Hogan's Alley; Åberg lurking at -4 despite putter change.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Six co-leaders at -6 after Round 1 of the Charles Schwab Challenge at Colonial:

Tom Kim | Lee Hodges | JJ Spaun
Matt McCarty | Ryan Gerard | Andrew Putnam

Åberg is 2 back at -4 after switching putters.

Round 2 is underway. Wide open. 🏌️

#CharlesSchwabChallenge #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
80 straight years at Colonial Country Club.

Six players share the R1 lead at -6.
Åberg, the betting favorite, is 2 back at -4.

Round 2 underway now. R3 Saturday. R4 Sunday.

Golf Channel / ESPN+

#CharlesSchwabChallenge #PGATour #HogansAlley

facebook.long_form:
SIX-WAY TIE AT THE TOP OF HOGAN'S ALLEY.

The 2026 Charles Schwab Challenge at Colonial Country Club is off to a wide-open start. Six players share the Round 1 lead at 6-under-par 64:

Tom Kim | Lee Hodges | JJ Spaun | Matt McCarty | Ryan Gerard | Andrew Putnam

Soft conditions from early-week rains made for scoring opportunities on a course that usually plays as one of the most demanding on the PGA Tour. Colonial's tight fairways and demanding approach angles typically punish any imprecision — but the weekend weather created a window that the field's best ball-strikers exploited.

Sitting two shots back at 4-under is Ludvig Åberg, the tournament's betting favorite, who switched putters heading into the week. He's two off the lead and very much in the mix.

Today's Round 2 is underway. Round 3 is Saturday, and the final round is Sunday — all on Golf Channel and ESPN+.

Tournament purse: $9.9 million. This is the 80th consecutive year at Colonial Country Club — the longest uninterrupted venue-event relationship in PGA Tour history.

Who do you like to win this week? Drop your pick below! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption:
SIX SHARE THE LEAD AT COLONIAL 🏌️
Tom Kim | Hodges | Spaun | McCarty | Gerard | Putnam — all at -6
Åberg -4 | R2 Underway | R4 Sunday
$9.9M purse | Colonial Country Club | Golf Channel #CharlesSchwabChallenge

stories.title: LIV Golf Korea R2 — Gooch Leads at -8, DeChambeau One Back
stories.angle: Gooch goes wire-to-wire bid begins — OKGC building on R1 team lead.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf Korea R2 leaderboard (Asiad CC, Busan):

1. Talor Gooch (OKGC) — -8
2. Bryson DeChambeau (Crushers) — -7
T3. Smith / Niemann / Vincent / Pieters / Howell III — -5

OKGC leads teams at -14.

Final rounds Sat + Sun. 🏌️

#LIVGolf #LIVGolfKorea

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Talor Gooch leads LIV Golf Korea at -8 after 2 rounds.

Bryson DeChambeau is one back at -7.

Round 3 tomorrow. Final round Sunday.

Can Gooch go wire-to-wire in Busan?

#LIVGolf #LIVGolfKorea

facebook.long_form:
TALOR GOOCH IS MAKING A RUN IN BUSAN.

After 36 holes of LIV Golf Korea at the Asiad Country Club in Busan, South Korea, Talor Gooch is in pole position to go wire-to-wire in LIV's first-ever event on Korean soil.

Gooch shot a blistering 7-under 63 in Round 2 to vault to the top of the leaderboard at 8 under — one clear of Bryson DeChambeau, who sits at 7 under with Crushers GC. Tied for third at 5 under are a strong group of five: Cameron Smith (Ripper GC), Joaquin Niemann (Torque GC), Scott Vincent (HyFlyers GC), Thomas Pieters (4Aces GC), and Charles Howell III (Crushers GC).

On the team side, Gooch's OKGC squad leads at 14 under.

Two rounds remain. Round 3 is Saturday, and the final round is Sunday. In a LIV format with 57 players and no cut, everything can change quickly — but Gooch's 63 has given him a platform that DeChambeau and the rest of the field will need to attack.

Can Gooch hold on and give OKGC a team and individual victory? Golf in South Korea — the country that gave us Se Ri Pak and a generation of elite golfers — will be watching closely.
facebook.image_caption:
LIV GOLF KOREA R2 LEADERBOARD 🏌️
1. Gooch (OKGC): -8 | 2. DeChambeau (Crushers): -7
T3: Smith / Niemann / Vincent / Pieters / Howell III: -5
OKGC team leads at -14 | Final rounds Sat + Sun #LIVGolf

article.headline: Gooch's 63 Puts Him in Command at LIV Golf Korea as DeChambeau Lurks

stories.title: Ludvig Åberg's Win Drought — Colonial His Best Shot Yet
stories.angle: The talented Swede is hunting his second 2026 win at a course that fits his game.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Ludvig Åberg has 6 top-10 finishes in his last 7 starts.

Zero wins.

He switched putters for the Charles Schwab Challenge — opened at -4 at Colonial, where he finished T3 in 2025.

Today's Round 2 could be the start of something.

#PGATour #CharlesSchwabChallenge

facebook.long_form:
ÅBERG HAS BEEN EVERYWHERE — EXCEPT THE WINNER'S CIRCLE.

Ludvig Åberg's 2026 PGA Tour season is one of the most frustrating success stories in the game right now. He won the Genesis Invitational earlier in the year — a marquee event victory that reminded everyone why he was one of golf's most celebrated young players coming out of Europe. And since then? Six top-10 finishes in seven starts. No wins.

His ball-striking is elite. His course management is advanced for his age. He's consistently putting himself in position — and consistently watching someone else walk off with the trophy.

This week at the Charles Schwab Challenge, Åberg switched putters. It's the kind of adjustment a player makes when he knows he's losing strokes on the greens and needs a change. He opened with a 4-under 66 at Colonial — two shots off the six-way lead — and he's here in a field that perfectly suits his strengths.

Why Colonial? The Green Mile closing stretch (holes 16-18) demands precision approach play and reliable putting — two things Åberg is actively working on. He finished T3 here in 2025. He knows the course, knows the demands, and knows what it takes to contend on a Sunday.

The win drought ends somewhere. Could it be here? Round 2 is underway. ⛳
facebook.image_caption:
6 TOP-10s. ZERO WINS. ⛳
Åberg switched putters for the Schwab Challenge — opened at -4 at Colonial.
He finished T3 here in 2025. This week could be different.
Round 2 underway | #PGATour #CharlesSchwabChallenge

article.headline: Åberg's Win Drought Reaches Six: Why Colonial Could Be His Breakthrough

stories.title: US Women's Open Preview — Riviera CC, June 4-7, All-Star Field
stories.angle: Historic venue, stacked field, Korda going for 2026 major No. 2 — marquee week for women's golf.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The US Women's Open is 6 days away.

June 4-7 | Riviera CC, Pacific Palisades, CA
First USGA women's major EVER at Riviera.

Korda (3 wins in 2026) is the favorite.
Thitikul (World No. 1) leads the field.
Stark defends.

Women's golf's biggest stage. 🏆

#USWomensOpen #LPGA

facebook.long_form:
RIVIERA. JUNE 4-7. THE BIGGEST STAGE IN WOMEN'S GOLF ARRIVES.

The 81st US Women's Open Presented by Ally will be held at Riviera Country Club in Pacific Palisades, California — and for the first time in its history, one of golf's most celebrated venues will host a USGA women's major championship.

Here's everything you need to know:

THE FIELD: All 25 of the world's top-ranked players are entered. World No. 1 Jeeno Thitikul headlines the official rankings. But Nelly Korda — who has won three times in 2026 (including the Chevron Championship, one of the LPGA's five major championships) — arrives as the tournament's hottest player and a clear favorite. Maja Stark, who won the 2025 US Women's Open at Erin Hills, returns to defend her title.

THE VENUE: Riviera Country Club is one of golf's most storied addresses, famous for its men's events and the PGA Tour's Genesis Invitational. The course rewards precision shotmaking and creative approaches — exactly the kind of golf that separates the great players from the very good.

THE STAKES: A US Women's Open title for Korda would be her second major in 2026 — and cement what would be one of the greatest individual LPGA seasons in recent memory. For Stark, defending a major title at a new venue is one of golf's ultimate challenges.

Tickets and streaming options: NBC/Peacock and Golf Channel. June 4-7. Don't miss it.
facebook.image_caption:
US WOMEN'S OPEN 2026 — RIVIERA CC 🏆
June 4-7 | Pacific Palisades, CA
First USGA women's major EVER at Riviera
Korda (3 wins), Thitikul (World No. 1), Stark (defending) | #USWomensOpen #LPGA

article.headline: US Women's Open Preview: Riviera Hosts First USGA Women's Major, Korda Targets Second 2026 Major

stories.title: Tom Kim Shines in Schwab R1 — Six-Under 64 Signals Form Return
stories.angle: Tom Kim rediscovering his best — and doing it at a course that rewards his strengths.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Tom Kim shot a 6-under 64 in Round 1 of the Charles Schwab Challenge.

He's tied for the lead at Colonial — and showing the form that made him one of the game's most exciting players in 2022.

Round 2 is live now. Eyes on Kim.

#PGATour #CharlesSchwabChallenge

facebook.long_form:
TOM KIM IS BACK. AT LEAST FOR ONE ROUND.

Tom Kim shot a 6-under 64 in Round 1 of the Charles Schwab Challenge at Colonial Country Club, co-leading the tournament after the opening round — and doing so in a way that reminded everyone watching why he was once considered one of the game's most promising young stars.

Kim burst onto the scene in 2022 with two PGA Tour victories, a high-energy game, and a personality that connected with fans around the world. He won the Shriners Children's Open and made a remarkable Ryder Cup debut for the United States. For a moment, it looked like the beginning of something special.

Since then, the wins have not come as easily. The game is harder at the top level than one great season can capture, and Kim has been searching to recapture his early brilliance with mixed results.

A 64 at Colonial — a course that rewards elite ball-striking and demands patience — is a genuine sign of life. The conditions were soft from early-week rain, which helped scoring, but Kim still had to execute at the highest level to reach 6-under.

Round 2 is underway right now. If Kim stays in the hunt through Friday, the weekend will be very interesting.
facebook.image_caption:
TOM KIM SHARES THE R1 LEAD AT COLONIAL ⛳
6-under 64 in Round 1 | Tied for the lead
"Showing signs of life" — CBS Sports
Round 2 underway | #PGATour #CharlesSchwabChallenge

article.headline: Tom Kim Shines in Schwab R1: A Six-Under 64 and Signs of a Return
